cards.title: Adaptogens — Evidence & Clinical Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
Definition (Brekhman & Dardymov, 1969)
An adaptogen must: (1) be non-toxic, (2) produce a non-specific resistance to stress, (3) have a normalising effect regardless of direction of pathological change — i.e. bidirectional modulation.
AdaptogenLatin NamePrimary ActionsBest EvidenceDose
AshwagandhaWithania somnifera↓ Cortisol, ↑ thyroid (T3/T4), ↑ testosterone, ↑ muscleAnxiety ★, fatigue, hypothyroid, male fertilityKSM-66: 300–600mg/day
Rhodiola roseaRhodiola rosea↑ Serotonin/dopamine, ↓ cortisol, ↑ mental performanceBurnout ★, mental fatigue, depression adjunctSHR-5: 200–400mg/day (3% rosavins)
Panax ginsengPanax ginseng↑ CNS stimulation, ↑ NK cells, ↑ NO, antidiabeticCognitive function ★, T2DM, fatigue, immune200–400mg (standardised 4% ginsenosides)
EleutheroEleutherococcus senticosus↑ Physical endurance, immune modulationAthletic performance, chronic fatigue300–1200mg/day
SchisandraSchisandra chinensisHepatoprotective, ↓ cortisol, ↑ mental enduranceLiver protection, stress, cognitive500–2000mg/day
Holy basil (Tulsi)Ocimum tenuiflorum↓ Cortisol, antibacterial, adaptogenic, ↓ blood sugarAnxiety, metabolic syndrome, BNYS-specific ★300–2000mg or 1–2 cups/day
🟡 Avoid adaptogens in
Pregnancy (most), autoimmune disease on immunosuppressants (ginseng, eleuthero stimulate immunity), hyperthyroidism (ashwagandha ↑ T3/T4), hormone-sensitive cancers (Panax ginseng has oestrogen-like activity). Rhodiola is stimulating — avoid in evening, bipolar, or with SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk at high dose).

cards.title: Herb-Drug Interactions — Critical Table
cards._legacyHtml:
High-risk combinations — know these
HerbDrug ClassInteractionMechanismRisk
St John's WortSSRIs, SNRIs↑ Serotonin syndrome↑ SerotoninHIGH ★
St John's WortWarfarin, OCP, HIV meds, cyclosporin↓ Drug levels → treatment failureCYP3A4 + P-gp inductionHIGH ★
Ginkgo bilobaWarfarin, aspirin, NSAIDs↑ Bleeding riskAntiplatelet effectHIGH ★
ValerianBenzodiazepines, barbiturates↑ CNS depressionGABA potentiationModerate
Garlic / Dong quaiWarfarin, antiplatelets↑ Bleeding riskAntiplatelet + anticoagulantModerate
Liquorice (DGL-free)Antihypertensives, corticosteroids↑ BP, ↓ K⁺, potentiates steroidsPseudoaldosteronism (glycyrrhizin)Moderate
EchinaceaImmunosuppressants (cyclosporin, tacrolimus)↓ Drug efficacyImmune stimulation opposesModerate
AshwagandhaThyroid hormones↑ T3/T4 → possible thyrotoxicosisTSH-lowering, T4→T3 conversion ↑Moderate
🟡 Rule of thumb
ANY herb with antiplatelet activity (garlic, ginger, turmeric, ginkgo, dong quai, fish oil >3g) → withhold 2 weeks before surgery. Always ask about herbs BEFORE prescribing. St John's Wort interacts with more drugs than any pharmaceutical except rifampicin — it must be avoided with any narrow therapeutic index drug.

cards.title: Cardiovascular & Anti-inflammatory Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbKey ActionsDoseEvidenceInteractions
Hawthorn (Crataegus) ★Positive inotrope, coronary vasodilator, antioxidant900mg extract/day★★★★ NYHA I–II heart failureDigoxin (additive), antihypertensives
Arjuna (Terminalia arjuna)Cardioprotective, reduces cardiac hypertrophy, improves EF500mg bark extract TDS★★★ Indian RCTs for angina, HFAntihypertensives, anticoagulants
Garlic (Allium sativum)BP ↓ 5–10mmHg, LDL ↓, antiplatelet, antimicrobialAged garlic extract 600–1200mg/day★★★ Multiple meta-analysesWarfarin (↑ bleeding), antiplatelets
Boswellia serrata ★5-LOX inhibitor — reduces LTB4. Anti-inflammatory. Pain relief.300–400mg TDS (65% boswellic acids)★★★ OA, asthma, IBD, RAAnticoagulants (mild)
Turmeric/Curcumin ★NF-kB inhibition, COX inhibition, antioxidant, anti-tumour500–1000mg + piperine 20mg BD★★★ Broad anti-inflammatory evidenceWarfarin (high dose), bile duct obstruction CI
Willow barkCOX inhibition (salicin — like aspirin), antipyretic, analgesic240mg salicin/day★★★ LBP, OAAspirin, NSAIDs, anticoagulants — additive
Ginger ★COX-1/2 inhibition, anti-emetic (5-HT3 antagonism), prokinetic1–3g dried ginger/day or 500mg extract BD★★★★ NVP — good for OA painWarfarin (mild antiplatelet)

cards.title: Antimicrobial, Digestive & Women's Health Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
ANTIMICROBIAL & DIGESTIVE HERBS
HerbActionsDoseNotes
Berberine ★Antimicrobial, antifungal, AMPK activator, antidiarrhoeal500mg TDS with mealsBroad: MRSA, H.pylori, Candida, Giardia. Avoid in pregnancy. Interacts with cyclosporin.
AndrographisAntiviral, immune-stimulant, hepatoprotective400mg TDS (6% andrographolides)URTI, influenza, dengue. Avoid in autoimmune, pregnancy.
Oil of OreganoAntimicrobial, antifungal (Candida), antiparasitic200mg carvacrol TDSRotate every 2 weeks (resistance). Avoid in pregnancy.
Neem ★Antimicrobial, antifungal, antiparasitic, antidiabetic, hepatoprotective500mg leaf extract BDAvoid in pregnancy, young children. May reduce fertility.
Triphala ★Gentle laxative, prebiotic, antioxidant, antimicrobial1–2g at nightVery safe — long-term use acceptable. Traditional Ayurvedic tonic.
DGL Liquorice ★Mucous membrane protection, anti-ulcer, anti-inflammatory380mg chewed before mealsDGL form (deglycyrrhizinated) — safe long-term. Whole root: raises BP.
Peppermint oil (enteric-coated)Antispasmodic (smooth muscle relaxation), carminative0.2–0.4mL enteric-coated capsule TDSIBS, SIBO. MUST be enteric-coated — otherwise causes GERD.
WOMEN'S HEALTH HERBS
HerbUseDoseEvidenceCautions
Vitex agnus-castus ★PMS, PMDD, irregular cycles, hyperprolactinaemia, mastalgia400–500mg OD morning★★★ Good — needs 3+ monthsAvoid with dopamine antagonists, OCP, pregnancy
Black cohoshMenopause hot flushes — NOT oestrogenic (serotonergic)40–80mg/day★★★ Reduces hot flushes 50%Monitor LFTs. Max 6 months. Safe in ER+ cancer.
Gymnema sylvestreReduces sugar craving, lowers blood glucose, insulin sensitiser400mg BD (25% gymnemic acids)★★★ T2DM adjunctMonitor glucose if on antidiabetics
Bitter melon (Karela)Blood glucose ↓ (charantin, vicine — insulin-like), PCOS50–100ml fresh juice OD or 500mg BD★★★ Multiple Indian RCTsAvoid in pregnancy. G6PD deficiency.

cards.title: Respiratory, Immune & Adaptogenic Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
RESPIRATORY HERBS
HerbActionsDoseBest ForCautions
Vasaka (Adhatoda vasica) ★Bronchodilator, expectorant, antispasmodic. Vasicine = precursor of bromhexine.500mg TDS or 10ml juice TDSAsthma, bronchitis, COPD, productive coughAvoid in pregnancy (uterotonic)
Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum) ★Adaptogen, immunomodulator, antiviral, bronchodilator, antistress500mg BD or 2 cups tea/dayURTI, mild asthma, stress, immune supportMild anticoagulant. Generally very safe.
ThymeExpectorant, antispasmodic, antimicrobial (thymol)100mg thymol extract TDS or 2 tsp herb tea TDSProductive cough, bronchitis, COPDGenerally safe at food doses
IMMUNE HERBS
HerbDoseEvidence
Echinacea ★E. purpurea 300mg TDS. Max 10 days continuous.★★★ Reduces URTI duration 1–4 days
Astragalus (Huang Qi)500mg extract BD or 4–7g root/day★★★ Immune modulator, antiviral, anti-fatigue, oncology adjunct
Elderberry15ml syrup QID during illness or 600mg extract/day★★★ Reduces influenza duration 3–4 days (meta-analysis)
ADAPTOGENS (HPA AXIS)
HerbDoseBest ForCautions
Ashwagandha KSM-66 ★300mg BDStress, burnout, thyroid, testosterone, sleep, PCOSAvoid in autoimmune, pregnancy. Nightshade family.
Rhodiola rosea ★200–400mg AM only (3% rosavins)Mental fatigue, burnout Stage 1–2, athletic performanceStimulating — avoid Stage 4 burnout, PM doses (insomnia)
Schisandra500mg extract BDLiver support, mental fatigue, hepatitis adjunctAvoid in pregnancy. CYP3A4 interactions.

cards.title: Cardiovascular & Anti-inflammatory Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbKey ActionsDoseEvidenceInteractions
Hawthorn (Crataegus)Positive inotrope, coronary vasodilator, antioxidant, antiarrhythmic900mg extract/dayStrong for NYHA I-II heart failureDigoxin (additive), antihypertensives
Arjuna (Terminalia arjuna)Cardioprotective, reduces hypertrophy, improves EF500mg bark extract TDSGood — Indian RCTs for angina, HFAntihypertensives (additive)
Garlic (Allium sativum)BP down 5-10mmHg, LDL down, antiplatelet, antimicrobialAged garlic extract 600-1200mg/dayGood — multiple meta-analysesWarfarin (bleeding risk), antiplatelets
Olive leaf (Oleuropein)BP down, antioxidant, antiviral500-1000mg/dayGood for hypertensionAntihypertensives (additive)
Boswellia serrata5-LOX inhibitor, reduces LTB4, potent anti-inflammatory300-400mg TDS (65% boswellic acids)Good for OA, asthma, IBD, RAAnticoagulants (mild)
Turmeric (Curcumin)NF-kB inhibition, COX inhibition, antioxidant500-1000mg with piperine 20mg BDGood — broad anti-inflammatory evidenceWarfarin at high doses
Willow bark (Salicin)COX inhibition (aspirin-like), antipyretic, analgesic240mg salicin/dayGood for LBP, OA painAspirin, NSAIDs, anticoagulants — additive
Ginger (Zingiber officinale)COX-1/2 inhibition, anti-emetic, prokinetic1-3g dried ginger/dayStrong for nausea, good for OA painWarfarin (mild antiplatelet)
🟡 Herb Safety Before Surgery
Stop all herbal medicines 2 weeks before elective surgery. Garlic, ginger, ginkgo, ginseng — antiplatelet effects. Hawthorn — cardiac effects. St John's Wort — CYP induction affects anaesthetic metabolism. Valerian, kava — potentiate anaesthetic agents. Always take a full herbal history at pre-operative assessment — most patients do not volunteer this information unless specifically asked.

cards.title: Antimicrobial & Digestive Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbKey ActionsDoseBest ForCautions
BerberineAntimicrobial, antifungal, antiparasitic, AMPK activator (metabolic)500mg TDS with mealsSIBO, H.pylori, Candida, T2DM, PCOSCyclosporin interaction. Avoid pregnancy. P450 inhibitor.
AndrographisAntiviral, immune-stimulant, anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective400mg TDS (6% andrographolides)Viral URTI, influenza, dengue supportAvoid autoimmune disease, pregnancy
Oil of Oregano (Carvacrol)Broad-spectrum antimicrobial, antifungal200mg carvacrol TDSCandida, SIBO, H.pylori adjunctRotate every 2 weeks to prevent resistance. Avoid pregnancy.
Neem (Azadirachta indica)Antimicrobial, antifungal, antiparasitic, anti-inflammatory500mg leaf extract BDGut dysbiosis, dental health, skin infectionsAvoid pregnancy, young children. Hepatotoxic in overdose.
TriphalaGentle laxative, prebiotic, antioxidant, hepatoprotective1-2g at nightConstipation, dysbiosis, digestive tonicVery safe — long-term use acceptable
DGL LiquoriceAnti-ulcer, mucous membrane protection, anti-inflammatory380mg chewed before mealsGERD, peptic ulcer, gastritis, oral mucositisDGL form is safe. Standard liquorice root raises BP — avoid long-term.
Peppermint oil (enteric-coated)Antispasmodic (L-menthol), carminative, antimicrobial0.2-0.4mL enteric-coated capsule TDSIBS, SIBO, intestinal spasm, bloatingMust be enteric-coated. Avoid in GERD without coating.
Dandelion rootCholeretic, diuretic, prebiotic (inulin), hepatoprotective500mg extract BD or 3 cups root tea/dayLiver support, constipation, bloating, UTI preventionAvoid bile duct obstruction. May interact with diuretics.

cards.title: Women's Health, Immune & Metabolic Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
WOMEN'S HEALTH HERBS
HerbPrimary UseDoseEvidenceCautions
Vitex agnus-castusPMS, PMDD, irregular cycles, mastalgia, hyperprolactinaemia400-500mg standardised extract OD (morning)Good — 3+ months for effectAvoid with dopamine antagonists. Avoid in pregnancy and OCP.
Black cohoshMenopausal hot flushes, mood, sleep — NOT oestrogenic40-80mg/dayGood — reduces hot flushes 50%Monitor LFTs long-term. Max 6 months then reassess.
Red raspberry leafUterine toning — traditional from 32 weeks gestation only1-2 cups tea/day from 32 weeksModerate — traditional, limited RCTAvoid before 32 weeks (uterotonic)
Dong quaiDysmenorrhoea, irregular cycles, blood tonic3-4.5g dried root/dayModeratePhotosensitising. Avoid pregnancy, heavy bleeding, anticoagulants.
IMMUNE & METABOLIC HERBS
HerbPrimary UseDoseEvidence
EchinaceaURTI prevention and treatment, NK cell activationE.purpurea 300mg TDS. Max 10 days continuous.Good — reduces URTI duration 1-4 days
Astragalus (Huang Qi)Immune modulator, adaptogen, antiviral, anti-fatigue500mg extract BDGood — traditional + emerging oncology evidence
Elderberry (Sambucus nigra)Antiviral (influenza, cold), immune stimulant15ml syrup QID during illness or 600mg extract/dayGood — reduces influenza duration 3-4 days
Gymnema sylvestreReduces sugar cravings, lowers blood glucose, improves insulin sensitivity400mg (25% gymnemic acids) BDGood for T2DM adjunct
Bitter melon (Karela)Blood glucose lowering, insulin-like action, PCOS support50-100ml fresh juice OD or 500mg extract BDGood — multiple Indian RCTs

cards.title: Respiratory & Adaptogenic Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
RESPIRATORY HERBS
HerbKey ActionsDoseBest ForCautions
Vasaka (Adhatoda vasica)Bronchodilator, expectorant, antispasmodic. Vasicine is precursor of bromhexine.500mg leaf extract TDS or 10ml juice TDSAsthma, bronchitis, COPD, productive coughAvoid in pregnancy (uterotonic). Not for dry cough.
Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum)Adaptogen, immunomodulator, antiviral, bronchodilator, COX-2 inhibitor500mg extract BD or 2 cups tea/dayRespiratory infections, asthma (mild), stress, immunityMild anticoagulant. Generally very safe.
Thyme (Thymus vulgaris)Expectorant, antispasmodic, antimicrobial (thymol)1-2 tsp dried herb as tea TDSProductive cough, bronchitis, COPDGenerally safe. Avoid high-dose essential oil internally.
Licorice rootExpectorant, demulcent, anti-inflammatory, antiviralDGL 380mg TDS for GI. Whole root 200mg TDS for respiratory (short-term only)Respiratory tract infections, laryngitis, bronchitisWhole root: raises BP, hypokalaemia — max 6 weeks.
ADAPTOGENS — HPA AXIS SUPPORT
HerbTypeDoseBest ForCautions
Ashwagandha KSM-66Adaptogen — bidirectional cortisol modulation300mg BDStress, burnout, thyroid, testosterone, sleep, PCOSAvoid in autoimmune disease, pregnancy. Nightshade family.
Rhodiola roseaAdaptogen — reduces fatigue, improves cognition200-400mg (3% rosavins) AM onlyMental fatigue, burnout Stage 1-2, athletic performanceStimulating — avoid Stage 4 burnout (low cortisol). Avoid PM (insomnia).
Siberian ginseng (Eleuthero)Adaptogen — immune support, anti-fatigue300-600mg extract/dayFatigue, immune support, cancer treatment adjunctAvoid in hypertension (high doses). Interferes with digoxin assay.
SchisandraHepatoprotective, adaptogen, cognitive, anti-fatigue500mg extract BDLiver support, mental fatigue, hepatitis adjunctAvoid pregnancy. CYP3A4 interactions.

cards.title: Hepatoprotective Herbs — Liver Support
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbActive CompoundsMechanismBest EvidenceDose
Milk ThistleSilymarin (silibinin)Antioxidant (↑ GSH), membrane stabiliser, anti-fibrotic, ↑ protein synthesisCirrhosis, hepatitis (viral, alcoholic), Amanita poisoning ★420mg/day silymarin
Dandelion rootTaraxacin, inulin, bitter sesquiterpenes↑ Bile flow, mild diuretic, prebiotic, anti-inflammatoryLiver congestion, constipation, UTI4–10g root/day or tincture
Globe artichokeCynarin, luteolin↑ Bile production/flow, hepatoprotective, lipid-loweringDyslipidaemia ★, NAFLD, dyspepsia320–640mg extract/day
Turmeric / CurcuminCurcuminoidsNF-κB ↓, COX-2 ↓, Nrf2 ↑, anti-fibroticNAFLD ★, IBD, arthritis, cancer adjunct500–2000mg/day + piperine or liposomal
AndrographisAndrographolideAnti-viral, NF-κB ↓, immune modulationViral hepatitis, URTI, IBD200–400mg andrographolide/day
🟡 Curcumin bioavailability — critical
Curcumin has only 1% oral bioavailability as standard powder. Solutions: (1) Piperine (black pepper extract, bioperine) 20mg increases absorption 2000%, (2) Liposomal/phospholipid complex (Meriva, BCM-95) 7–29× increase, (3) Nanoparticle formulations. Always specify the formulation when recommending. BCM-95 or Meriva at 500mg/day equals 8–10g standard curcumin powder.

cards.title: Nervines & Neurological Herbs
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbCategoryMechanismBest UseDose
AshwagandhaAdaptogen-nervine↓ Cortisol, GABA-A modulation, BDNF ↑Anxiety + fatigue, adrenal fatigueKSM-66 300mg twice daily
ValerianSedative nervineGABA transaminase inhibition, GABA-A agonismInsomnia ★, anxiety, muscle tension400–900mg 30min before bed
PassionflowerAnxiolytic nervineGABA-A potentiation (similar to benzos, no dependence)Anxiety ★, insomnia, withdrawal support250–500mg or tincture
Lemon balmRelaxing nervineMAO inhibition (mild), GABA-T inhibition, antiviralAnxiety, insomnia, cold sores, ADHD (children)300–600mg (or tea)
Bacopa monnieriNootropic↑ Acetylcholine, antioxidant, ↑ BDNF, adaptogenicMemory ★, ADHD adjunct, cognitive ageing300mg/day standardised (must take 8–12 weeks)
Lion's ManeNootropic mushroomNGF (nerve growth factor) stimulation, remyelinationCognitive decline ★, neuropathy, depression500–3000mg/day extract
🟡 Lion's Mane & NGF
Hericium erinaceus (Lion's Mane) is the only known food that stimulates NGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis in the brain. NGF promotes neuroplasticity, remyelination, and neurogenesis. RCT evidence for mild cognitive impairment (Mori et al., 2009 — 50% cognitive improvement vs placebo over 16 weeks). Requires 8 weeks minimum. Use hot-water extract (fruiting body) not mycelium for NGF-stimulating hericenones and erinacines.
